Execution · MVP control plane

MVP Delivery Tracker

The inspectable promise-to-evidence record for the complete character-to-Godot MVP.

TL;DR

5 promises, 10 scenarios, 25 user capabilities, 12 task flows, and 37 objective behaviors define the positive scope. 20 plausible adjacent expectations are deliberately excluded. Current stage: verified.

Canonical source

This page is generated from delivery/mvp/traceability.json. Status becomes verified only from actual-UI run evidence.

Customer promises

CP-QUICK-START

A developer can begin with a useful animated character instead of an empty art canvas.

verified

CP-CREATIVE-CONTROL

A developer can make the character fit their game while remaining visually coherent.

verified

CP-REPRODUCIBLE-WORK

A developer can leave and return without losing the recipe or receiving a different character.

verified

CP-GAME-READY-DELIVERY

A developer can take the character into a game without manually slicing frames or reverse-engineering animation metadata.

verified

CP-LEGAL-CONFIDENCE

A developer can understand and carry forward the provenance and attribution attached to the chosen art.

verified

Objective scope counts

25

User-can statements

12

Actual task flows

37

Behavior oracles

5

Observed anomalies

Task-flow status

IDUser completionStatus
TF-FIRST-PROJECTNamed project with starter renderverified
TF-COMPOSEComplete visible recipeverified
TF-EDIT-COMPOSITIONEdited recipe with accurate status and provenanceverified
TF-THEMEResolved project theme and optional character overrideverified
TF-PREVIEWEvery required animation and direction inspectedverified
TF-SAVE-REOPENReloaded project has identical state and renderverified
TF-EXPORT-GENERICGeneric package downloaded and inspectedverified
TF-EXPORT-GODOTGodot package downloaded and fixture-validatedverified
TF-INSPECT-CREDITSVisible credits exactly match selected sourcesverified
TF-RECOVERInvalid state is blocked then explicitly restoredverified
TF-SECOND-PACKSecond pack follows the same complete contractverified
TF-KEYBOARD-JOURNEYGeneric export command reached without pointerverified

Deliberate exclusions

IDExpectationReasonDisposition
OUT-TERRAINGenerate terrain and autotilesReasonable for the North Star studio but not required to prove the complete character-to-engine promisePhase 5 second producer
OUT-STRUCTURESGenerate buildings, structures, and propsRequires a distinct producer and perspective constraints unrelated to the first character journeyPhase 5 after terrain
OUT-SCENECompose full scenesOne character does not need a scene graph to reach GodotPhase 5 when multiple producers exist
OUT-PIXEL-EDITOREdit or draw pixels manuallyThe promise is selection and semantic recoloring, not replacing a dedicated pixel editorPhase 6
OUT-ANIMATION-AUTHORINGCreate or change animation framesMVP consumes declared pack animations and only previews themPhase 6
OUT-MATERIAL-MAPSGenerate normal, specular, or emissive mapsUnlit character delivery is complete without speculative material inferencePhase 6 after manual editing
OUT-MOUNTSBuild mounts or rider pairingsRequires different frames, masks, and compositing rules that do not prove the humanoid MVPPhase 4 after pack contract validation
OUT-CREATURESBuild animals or non-humanoid creaturesDifferent animation contracts should be learned from a later real implementationPhase 4
OUT-ADVANCED-ANIMATIONSPreview spellcast, thrust, shoot, slash, hurt, run, climb, or jumpIdle and walk are sufficient to prove four-direction animation, export, and engine importPost-MVP content expansion
OUT-WEAPONSUse oversize animated weaponsOversize sheets introduce frame geometry not needed by the first two animationsPhase 4
OUT-CUSTOM-PACK-AUTHORINGCreate, install, or validate user-authored packsA second built-in pack proves the data boundary before exposing authoring UXPhase 4
OUT-UNITYExport native Unity assetsGodot is the deliberately chosen engine proof and generic JSON remains portablePhase 4 export adapter
OUT-LIVE-LINKAutomatically update a running engine projectA deterministic downloadable Godot package closes the MVP integration promise without filesystem permissions or pluginsPhase 6
OUT-CLI-APIDrive generation through a public CLI or APIThe headless core is architecturally callable but the customer MVP is the interactive journeyPhase 6
OUT-BULKGenerate many randomized NPCsBulk policies and uniqueness controls are a separate user jobPost-MVP automation stage
OUT-CLOUDSync projects through an account or cloud serviceLocal persistence satisfies return use without identity, infrastructure, or privacy scopeNorth Star
OUT-COLLABORATIONShare or co-edit projectsThe target user is solo/small-team and collaboration does not affect character correctnessNorth Star
OUT-MARKETPLACEBrowse or purchase community contentPack consumption must prove stable before distribution economicsAfter open pack ecosystem
OUT-PROJECT-LIBRARYManage several saved projects in an in-app libraryOne named local project proves create, rename, save, reload, recovery, and deterministic rebuild without adding library lifecycle behaviorPost-MVP project-management stage
OUT-MULTIPLE-CHARACTERSManage several character recipes inside one projectOne complete character is sufficient to prove pack composition, project theming, persistence, export, and provenance end to endPost-MVP project-management stage

Review the complete record

The repository delivery folder contains the exhaustive customer-promise.md, scenarios.md, capabilities.md, task-flows.md, experience-architecture.md, scope-ledger.md, decisions, issues, execution plan, and evidence runs.
